expressions.meaning: to pull up seedlings to help them grow — spoiling things through excessive impatience or haste
to have witnessed or experienced many changes and hardships in life; weathered and battle-hardened
extensive and profound — typically describing a culture, discipline, or body of knowledge
by fair means or foul; using any available method without scruples
inconceivable, unimaginable; beyond comprehension
the loss outweighs the gain; not worth the cost
so engrossed as to forget eating and sleeping; total absorption in a task
each person freely expresses their own opinion; open discussion
to attract worldwide attention; drawing the eyes of the world
unprecedented and unrepeatable; without parallel in history or the future
experienced and knowledgeable; well-versed through exposure
careful calculation and strict budgeting; meticulous planning of resources
with great relish; eagerly enjoying something (food, story, task)
with utmost caution and care; gingerly, on tiptoe
most willingly; to do something gladly of one's own accord
metabolism; the renewal of the old by the new (used literally for biology and figuratively for social/cultural change)
to achieve two goals with one action; kill two birds with one stone
as usual; exactly as always, unchanged
in perfect order; methodically, without a trace of confusion
to grow with each passing day; continuously increasing
to rely on one's own efforts; self-reliance and self-regeneration
to vie to be first, afraid of falling behind; competitive scramble
as everyone knows; it is common knowledge that…
expressions.register: 成语／书面语，常用于文章开头

expressions.meaning: happiness consists in contentment; those who know enough are always happy
listless, in low spirits; without energy or enthusiasm
helpless, powerless; unable to do anything about a situation
carefree, free from all anxieties; untouched by worry
to suddenly see the light; to realize all at once after confusion
to gaze around in all directions; looking about restlessly
expressions.register: 成语，常带贬义（心不在焉）

expressions.meaning: full of youthful vigor and vitality; bursting with energy
admittedly; it is true that… (concessive conjunction to acknowledge a counterpoint before asserting one's main claim)
expressions.register: 书面语连词，议论文常用

expressions.meaning: and then; furthermore; thereby (additive/consequential connector moving argument forward)
in view of; in light of; given that (formal causal/contextual preposition to introduce grounds for an argument)
to such an extent that; so … that (introduces an undesirable consequence)
so that; in order to (introduces purpose clause in formal writing)
nothing but; no more than; simply (minimizing what appears complex)
there is no harm in; might as well (softly proposing an action or suggestion)
can't help (doing something); unable to refrain from (marks spontaneous emotional reaction)
with the aid of; by means of; drawing support from (formal instrumental phrase)
to expound; to set forth in detail (formal verb for analytical writing)
to present; to take on (a certain appearance); to emerge (formal descriptive verb)
to change over time; to evolve; to undergo transformation (diachronic change, formal)
to involve; to relate to; to touch on (formal verb introducing the scope of a topic)
self-cultivation; personal refinement; cultural attainment (key concept in Chinese essay culture)
expressions.register: 书面语名词，文化核心词

expressions.meaning: local conditions and customs; the natural and social environment of a place
general mood; prevailing atmosphere; social ethos (formal social commentary term)
disadvantage; drawback; malpractice (formal term for problem analysis in argumentative essays)
quality; overall caliber (of a person or group); fundamental competence
expressions.register: 书面语名词，社会评论常用

expressions.meaning: to explore; to seek; to investigate (used both literally and figuratively in formal writing)

essay_takeaways: The vocabulary register throughout this chunk is predominantly formal written Chinese (书面语). At HSK 6, essays are expected to employ formal connectives (固然、进而、鉴于、以致、以便) rather than their colloquial equivalents — mastering these connectors is the single biggest lever for lifting essay register.

Four-character idioms (成语) should appear organically, not forced. The density of chengyu in this vocabulary set (at least 25 distinct ones) signals that HSK 6 model essays use them as precision tools: each replaces a full clause while adding rhetorical weight. Pick 2-4 well-chosen chengyu per essay rather than scattering many.

Chinese analytical essays frequently use a concessive-then-assert structure signaled by 固然…但/然而. Practice: '固然X有其合理性，但Y才是问题的关键。' This acknowledges the opposing view before pivoting, which is valued as balanced reasoning.

The formal verbs (阐述、呈现、演变、涉及、探索) are preferred over their common-register equivalents (说、变成、有关) in HSK 6 writing. Building a habit of substituting these in revision will immediately elevate the written register.

This chunk is almost entirely a vocabulary index with severely corrupted prose OCR — no model essays or grammar patterns were recoverable from the text. The essay_takeaways above are derived from vocabulary register analysis, not from observed essay structures in this file.
